meta.name: AASB / IFRS disclosure checklist
meta.basis: Australian Accounting Standards (AASB, IFRS-equivalent) and Corporations Act 2001 / ASX Listing Rules. Mirrors the structure of Accurri 'Example Financial Statements' (Pinnacle) model accounts.
meta.detection_note: Presence is screened by case-insensitive keyword match on the extracted report text. This is a FIRST-PASS SCREEN, not assurance. 'expectedWhen' makes an item conditional: if none of its trigger phrases appear, the item is treated as Not Applicable (a business-model difference, not a gap).

items.title: Presentation basis & rounding ($'000 vs full dollars)
items.recommendation: State the presentation currency and rounding basis (Corporations Instrument) clearly.

items.title: Restatement of comparatives note (where prior periods restated)
items.recommendation: If comparatives were restated/reclassified, disclose nature, amount and reason per AASB 108.

items.title: Critical judgements, estimates and assumptions note
items.recommendation: Provide a dedicated critical-judgements-and-estimates note consolidating significant judgements (e.g. control assessments, asset-acquisition vs business-combination, recoverability of receivables).
items.representationNote: Usually a dedicated 'Critical accounting judgements and key sources of estimation' note — when present, treat as addressed.

items.title: Interim financial reporting basis statement
items.recommendation: State that the interim report is prepared in accordance with AASB 134 Interim Financial Reporting.

items.title: Revenue disaggregation (category, geography, timing of transfer)
items.recommendation: Confirm revenue disaggregation meets AASB 15 ¶114-115. It need not be a single "disaggregation of revenue" table — it may be satisfied across the operating-segment note (revenue by product/segment), the geographic revenue split, and the timing of transfer in the revenue policy (point-in-time vs over-time). Verify the ¶115 link between disaggregated revenue and segment disclosures is explained.
items.representationNote: Commonly represented differently from the standard’s illustrative table: look in the operating-segment note, the geographic revenue note, and the revenue-recognition policy. Presence of these together generally satisfies ¶114-115 for a single-stream entity.

items.title: Contract assets / contract liabilities movement
items.recommendation: Disclose opening/closing contract balances and revenue recognised from prior-period contract liabilities.

items.title: Numerical income-tax reconciliation (even where tax is nil)
items.recommendation: Provide the prima-facie-to-actual income-tax reconciliation; required even for loss-makers with nil expense (AASB 112.81(c)).
items.representationNote: Usually a numerical reconciliation within the income-tax note (prima facie tax to actual) — when present, treat as addressed.

items.title: Unrecognised deferred tax assets (tax losses)
items.recommendation: Disclose unrecognised deferred tax assets and unused tax losses (AASB 112.81(e)).

items.title: Reconciliation of profit/(loss) to net operating cash flows
items.recommendation: Include a reconciliation of profit/(loss) after tax to net cash from operating activities. Common in annuals; often omitted in interims.
items.representationNote: Usually a 'reconciliation of profit/(loss) after tax to net cash from operating activities' in the cash-flow notes — when present, treat as addressed.

items.title: Non-cash investing & financing activities note
items.recommendation: Disclose material non-cash investing/financing items (e.g. consideration shares, performance rights, loan conversions) per AASB 107.43.
items.representationNote: Material non-cash items (consideration shares, performance rights, loan/option conversions) are commonly disclosed in the statement of changes in equity and the issued-capital note rather than a dedicated AASB 107 ¶43 note — confirm they are captured there.

items.title: Basic AND diluted EPS presented (separate lines)
items.recommendation: Present basic and diluted EPS/LPS as separate lines even where equal (anti-dilutive); state why potential shares are anti-dilutive (AASB 133).
items.representationNote: Often a single 'basic and diluted' line plus an explicit statement that potential shares are anti-dilutive and excluded (AASB 133 ¶43), rather than two separate lines — confirm the anti-dilutive explanation is present.

items.title: Share-based payments note (valuation inputs, vesting)
items.recommendation: Disclose SBP arrangements, valuation model and inputs, and amounts recognised (AASB 2).

items.standard: AASB 3 vs AASB 2 / IFRS 3 vs IFRS 2
items.title: Acquisition accounting basis (business combination vs asset acquisition)
items.recommendation: State whether each acquisition is a business combination (AASB 3) or an asset acquisition (allocate cost, no goodwill). Templates often assume AASB 3 only.

items.title: Intangible assets note (recognition, amortisation, impairment)
items.recommendation: Disclose intangible carrying values, amortisation method/useful life, and impairment testing (AASB 138 / AASB 136).

items.title: Financial instruments classification & measurement
items.recommendation: Disclose classification and measurement basis for financial assets/liabilities (AASB 9).

items.title: Expected credit loss / receivables ageing
items.recommendation: Provide ECL model, credit-risk and ageing analysis for receivables/loans (AASB 7.35). Often thin for small balances.

items.title: Fair value measurement hierarchy (Level 1/2/3)
items.recommendation: Where items are measured/disclosed at fair value (e.g. a loan discounted to fair value), give the AASB 13 Level 1/2/3 hierarchy and valuation inputs, not just a fair-value reference.

items.title: Leases — right-of-use assets and lease liabilities
items.recommendation: Disclose ROU assets, lease liabilities, maturity analysis and lease expense (AASB 16).

items.title: Non-controlling interests (incl. deficit NCI)
items.recommendation: Where NCI exists, attribute losses in full even into deficit, and disclose subsidiaries and ownership (AASB 10 / AASB 12).

items.title: Operating segments note
items.recommendation: Disclose reportable segments, the basis of identification, and segment revenue/result/assets (AASB 8).

items.title: Related party transactions
items.recommendation: Disclose related-party transactions and KMP compensation (AASB 124).

items.title: Commitments and contingencies
items.recommendation: Disclose capital/lease commitments and contingent assets/liabilities (AASB 137).

items.title: Events after the reporting period
items.recommendation: Disclose material post-balance-date events (AASB 110).

items.title: Parent entity information
items.recommendation: Provide supplementary parent-entity financial information (Corporations Regulations 2M.3.01).

items.title: Consolidated entity disclosure statement (CEDS)
items.recommendation: Include the Consolidated Entity Disclosure Statement (entities, type, place of incorporation, tax residency) for years ending on/after 30 June 2024.

items.title: Remuneration report (audited)
items.recommendation: Include an audited remuneration report covering principles, details, service agreements, share-based comp and the AGM vote (s300A).

items.title: Auditor's independence declaration & non-audit services
items.recommendation: Include the s307C independence declaration and non-audit-services disclosure.

items.title: Shareholder information (distribution, top 20, substantial holders)
items.recommendation: Include ASX shareholder information: distribution schedule, twenty largest holders, substantial holders, voting rights, unmarketable parcels (LR 4.10).

items.title: Appendix 4D (interim) / 4E (annual) cover
items.recommendation: Include the Appendix 4D/4E cover: results for announcement, NTA backing, dividends, control gained/lost, associates, audit/review status.

items.title: Going concern assessment (esp. for loss-makers)
items.recommendation: For loss-making / cash-burning entities, disclose the going-concern assessment, runway and any material uncertainties (AASB 101.25).
items.representationNote: Usually a 'Going concern' note within the basis of preparation — when present, treat as addressed.
